sanskrit:
सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम्।
असक्तं सर्वभृत् चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च।।१३.१५।।
transliteration: sarvendriya-guṇābhāsaṃ sarvendriya-vivarjitam | asaktaṃ sarva-bhṛc caiva nirguṇaṃ guṇa-bhoktṛ ca ||13.15||
literal_meaning: The Supersoul is the light of all senses yet devoid of senses, unattached yet sustainer of all, beyond the guṇas yet their experiencer.
contextual_meaning: The psyche’s core witness is simultaneously intimate and transcendent—source of every perception yet never trapped in perception, sustaining every role yet never identified with any role.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance that invites a shift from ego-identification to witness-position; the felt-sense that 'I am not my thoughts, yet my thoughts arise in me.'
trigger_scenario: You’re on Zoom, camera off, multitasking. Suddenly you see yourself from outside—watching the watcher who is watching the screen. A moment of vertigo: who is the one seeing the seer?
anchor_text: Witness life’s play without grasping its props.
